name: Banded Standing Glute Kickback
resistance_profile: Band tension builds as the hip extends, peaking the glute contraction in the shortened position — a contraction-biased single-leg isolation.
cues: Squeeze the glute to move the leg, don't swing it
Keep the torso still and the stance leg braced
Reach full hip extension with no lower-back arch
execution_steps: Anchor a loop band low or loop it around both ankles, and hold a rack or wall for balance.
Take tension on the working leg with a slight hip hinge and a soft stance-leg knee.
Drive the working leg straight back, extending the hip and squeezing the glute at the top.
Stop before the lower back arches — the movement comes from the hip, not the spine.
Control the leg back to the start against the band, then complete the reps and switch sides.
good_when: You want isolated glute contraction and mind-muscle work
You only have a band (home or travel)
You want single-leg work to address side-to-side differences
bad_when: You want a heavy mass-driving movement (band load is limited)
You dislike balancing on one leg for reps
common_errors: Arching the lower back to gain more range
Using momentum instead of a controlled glute squeeze
Rotating the hips open to cheat the leg backward
progressions: Use a thicker band or move it to the ankles
Add a pause at full extension
